title: Rhabdophyllum arnoldianum
taxonomy: Rhabdophyllum arnoldianum is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is also known as the Arnold's rhabdophyllum. This plant is a climber that grows in the tropical forests of Madagascar. Rhabdophyllum arnoldianum is classified into the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and division Magnoliophyta.

Traditionally, Rhabdophyllum arnoldianum has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
